title: Flavorful Canadian Red Onion En Croute
description: A savory Canadian dish featuring caramelized red onions in a flaky pastry crust, perfect for a low-sodium date night dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit
In a large skillet, caramelize the red onions over medium heat for 20 minutes
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and cold butter until a crumbly mixture forms
Gradually add the ice water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until a dough forms
Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/8 inch
In a small bowl, mix together the grated cheddar cheese, chopped thyme, and salt-free seasoning blend
Spread the caramelized red onions over the center of the dough, leaving a 1 inch border
Sprinkle the cheese mixture over the red onions
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg
Fold the dough over the filling, pressing the edges to seal
Place the en croute on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with the unsalted butter
